518692.name: Freddie Freeman
518692.background: Freddie Freeman is a former NL MVP and World Series champion who has been one of the most consistent first basemen in baseball for over a decade, a Dodger cornerstone whose contact, on-base skills, and clutch hitting make him one of the most valuable fantasy assets available. The Portland Chinook have him as their premier offensive weapon alongside Turner and Olson, counting on his elite batting average and consistent power to anchor their lineup from the cleanup spot. Freeman's career-long consistency makes him one of the safest keepers in the CBA.

518876.name: Merrill Kelly
518876.background: A durable contact-management specialist for the Diamondbacks, Merrill Kelly has been one of Arizona's most reliable workhorses — generating groundballs, limiting walks, and consistently posting ERA figures in the mid-3.00s through elite command and an efficient four-pitch mix. Available in CBA, he profiles as a floor-heavy streaming starter whose groundball approach, low walk rate, and dependable innings make him a useful plug when you need ratios help.

519242.name: Chris Sale
519242.background: Chris Sale is one of the most decorated lefties of his generation, a tall, whippy strike-thrower who spent years as an elite Red Sox ace before injuries derailed his career — now with the Braves, he's recaptured his elite form and reminded everyone why he was once the most feared pitcher in the AL. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms landed him as their staff ace, and when Sale is healthy and dealing he gives the Fuzzy Bottoms a top-shelf SP1 capable of shutting down any lineup. His strikeout ability remains elite.

519317.name: Giancarlo Stanton
519317.background: A veteran power bat on the Sugar Land Space Cowboys roster, Giancarlo Stanton has been one of the most physically imposing hitters in baseball history — an NL MVP whose raw power generates some of the highest exit velocities and longest home runs the sport has ever measured. His injury history has been extensive, but when healthy he's a .250/30-HR threat who changes the tenor of any lineup. The Space Cowboys have kept him on their roster knowing that even a partial season of Stanton's power can swing CBA matchups.
519317.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .256/.724 OPS, 3 HR, 14 RBI · currently on D10 IL

521692.name: Salvador Perez
521692.background: A beloved veteran and Kansas City legend on the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls roster, Salvador Perez is a six-time All-Star and World Series MVP whose durability and power from behind the plate have made him one of the most accomplished catchers of his generation. Now in his mid-30s, he provides a reliable floor with 20-homer upside and consistent RBI production for the Pepperoni Rolls' lineup. West Virginia counts on his professional leadership and power bat to anchor their catcher slot.

542303.name: Marcell Ozuna
542303.background: A veteran free agent DH/outfielder in CBA who still carries legitimate power, Marcell Ozuna has been one of the most prolific home run hitters in the NL throughout his career — posting multiple 35-plus homer seasons with Atlanta and producing RBI totals that consistently rank among the best in the league. Available for any CBA team looking to add DH power, he profiles as a .250-hitting right-handed bat with 25-HR upside when playing regularly.

543135.name: Nathan Eovaldi
543135.background: Nathan Eovaldi is a veteran right-hander for the Rangers with elite velocity and a fearless demeanor, a power pitcher who has remained one of the most reliable SP2/SP3 options in fantasy when healthy. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him pairing with Bryan Woo as their top two starters, giving them a rotation that can dominate matchups with pure arm strength. Eovaldi's consistency and ability to eat innings make him a valuable asset for the Sky Chiefs in a format that rewards starter volume.

543243.name: Sonny Gray
543243.background: A veteran rotation piece for the Portland Chinook, Sonny Gray is a craftsman who has reinvented himself multiple times over a long career — most recently as a groundball-heavy, command-oriented starter whose mature pitch mix and exceptional competitiveness have kept him productive well into his 30s. His Cardinals tenure produced some of the best work of his career, and Portland has relied on his steadiness and durability to provide innings at the top of their rotation. He's the kind of reliable veteran every CBA team covets.

543257.name: Randal Grichuk
543257.background: A veteran right-handed power hitter, Randal Grichuk has been a useful fantasy piece as a fourth outfielder who can produce 20 HR when given extended playing time. He's a free swinger with below-average contact and walk rates but above-average raw power, making him a boom-or-bust streaming option when someone goes down in Arizona's outfield.
543257.recentAnalysis: Grichuk is struggling with a .194/.212/.323 line through 33 PA — no home runs and minimal walks suggest he's in a slump and not currently a productive roster asset.

543510.name: James McCann
543510.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .203/.471 OPS, 0 HR, 6 RBI · currently on D10 IL

543685.name: Anthony Rendon
543685.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

543760.name: Marcus Semien
543760.background: A veteran presence and leader in the Sugar Land Space Cowboys clubhouse, Marcus Semien is a two-time All-Star whose elite defensive metrics at shortstop are matched by consistent 25-30 homer power and reliable run production at the top of a lineup. His Rangers tenure produced back-to-back elite offensive seasons before age began to temper the counting stats, but he remains a useful contributor with excellent durability. The Space Cowboys rely on his professionalism and floor production as part of their veteran core.

543807.name: George Springer
543807.background: A prized veteran on the Warren Whistlepigs roster, George Springer is a former World Series MVP and five-time All-Star who brings elite leadoff skills, 25-plus homer power, and legitimate stolen base speed. His transition to Toronto was followed by multiple injury-shortened seasons, but when healthy he remains a top-20 outfielder with the full five-category profile. The Whistlepigs have kept faith through his injury history because the ceiling remains undeniably elite.

545121.name: Ildemaro Vargas
545121.background: Ildemaro Vargas is a veteran utility infielder who has carved out a long career through defensive versatility and a reliable contact approach. At his age he's a role player, but he's shown surprising pop late in his career and contributes positively when given regular playing time. His value in fantasy is almost entirely situational based on playing time.

545341.name: Randal Grichuk
545341.background: Randal Grichuk is a veteran outfielder known for his raw power and solid defense in right field, though a persistent high strikeout rate has kept his batting average suppressed throughout his career. He hit 20-plus home runs multiple times in his prime and remains capable of power contributions in a platoon or part-time role. His fantasy value has diminished with age but he's a reliable bench piece with HR upside in the right situation.

545361.name: Mike Trout
545361.background: The greatest player in West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls history and arguably the best baseball player of his generation, Mike Trout is a three-time MVP whose combination of elite power, speed, on-base skills, and defense defined the template for five-category dominance for over a decade. Injury has limited his recent availability, but West Virginia has held his rights through it all — knowing that even a partial season of Trout contributes at an elite level across every offensive category. When healthy, there is no better player in CBA.

547184.name: Michael Kelly
547184.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 6.23 ERA/2.31 WHIP, 4 K · currently on RM IL

547973.name: Aroldis Chapman
547973.background: The closer for the Bristol Banshees and a legendary figure in baseball history, Aroldis Chapman has thrown the fastest pitches ever recorded — routinely hitting 105-106 mph — and has converted saves at an elite rate throughout a career that has produced multiple World Series appearances and All-Star selections. Now in the veteran chapter of his career, he brings experience, a still-dangerous fastball, and professional save conversion ability to Bristol's ninth inning. The Banshees have built their bullpen identity around his late-game dominance.

553993.name: Eugenio Suarez
553993.background: A power-hitting third baseman for the Bristol Banshees, Eugenio Suarez has been one of the most reliable home run hitters in the NL for nearly a decade — posting six consecutive 20-plus HR seasons with a free-swinging approach that sacrifices average for power. The Banshees have leaned on his pop and RBI production to stay competitive in the power categories, and he provides the kind of 25-30 HR floor that is difficult to replicate at third base in CBA. He bats in the middle of Seattle's lineup with consistent RBI opportunities.

554340.name: Yimi Garcia
554340.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

554430.name: Zack Wheeler
554430.background: Zack Wheeler is a former Cy Young Award winner and one of the most dominant power pitchers in baseball, a Phillies ace whose triple-digit fastball and devastating slider have made him an annual fantasy SP1 when healthy. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys grabbed him as their co-ace alongside Max Fried, and when Wheeler is pitching at full strength there's no more imposing arm in the CBA. His strikeout rate and ground-ball ability make him truly elite on the days he takes the mound.

571448.name: Nolan Arenado
571448.background: A veteran cornerstone and future Hall of Famer on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster, Nolan Arenado is the greatest defensive third baseman of his generation — a ten-time Gold Glove winner whose elite glovework is matched by consistent 25-30 HR, 90-RBI seasons in the middle of a productive lineup. His Cardinals tenure has been defined by continued offensive excellence and postseason leadership, and Ft. Meade has relied on his floor production to stay competitive in the power and RBI categories year after year.

571510.name: Matthew Boyd
571510.background: Matthew Boyd is a crafty lefty who has overcome multiple major injuries — including Tommy John surgery — to re-establish himself as a reliable starting option. He relies on deception, a solid changeup, and command rather than velocity, making him strikeout-capable against lefty-heavy lineups. His value in fantasy is tied to health and innings accumulation, and he's proven he can still post quality start and strikeout totals when fully healthy.
571510.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 6.00 ERA/1.29 WHIP, 31 K · currently on D15 IL

571657.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .286/.624 OPS, 0 HR, 3 RBI · currently on D10 IL

571946.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

571970.name: Max Muncy
571970.background: A free agent first baseman in CBA with elite power and walks, Max Muncy has been one of the most dangerous left-handed hitters in the NL for the Dodgers — combining 35-homer upside with one of the best walk rates in baseball to produce elite OBP figures year after year. Available to any CBA team looking to add first base power, he profiles as a .240-hitting first baseman with 25-plus home runs and outstanding on-base skills. His CBA value is concentrated in HR, walks, and runs.

572122.name: J.P. Crawford
572122.background: A patient left-handed hitting shortstop for Seattle, J.P. Crawford has built his value around elite walk rates and solid defensive play rather than power or speed. His OBP has consistently been 40+ points above his batting average, making him more valuable in OBP leagues. He provides batting average stability without home run or stolen base production.
572122.recentAnalysis: Crawford is hitting .186/.284/.332 through 87 PA — a slow start with limited power, though his walk rate suggests the batting average will recover. Not currently worth starting in standard leagues.

572233.name: Christian Walker
572233.background: A first base cornerstone for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls, Christian Walker has been one of the most underrated first basemen in the NL — consistently posting 30-plus homer seasons with elite defense and solid on-base skills in Arizona's lineup. His combination of power, walks, and run production has made him a reliable CBA contributor year after year, and the Pepperoni Rolls have counted on his floor production to anchor their corner infield slot. Walker's consistency is as dependable as any player on their roster.

575929.name: Willson Contreras
575929.background: The Delmarva Emus' starting catcher and one of their most passionate contributors, Willson Contreras has been one of the most productive catchers in the NL for the better part of a decade — combining 20-plus homer power with excellent arm strength and a take-charge presence behind the plate. His St. Louis tenure has produced continued offensive production in a lineup built around his RBI opportunities. For Delmarva, he's the rare CBA catcher who can legitimately anchor a starting lineup slot.

579328.name: Yusei Kikuchi
579328.background: A rotation piece for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets, Yusei Kikuchi arrived from Japan with a plus arsenal that has produced multiple strikeout-heavy seasons for Seattle and Toronto. His fastball-slider combination is effective against left-handed hitters in particular, and the Ferrets have relied on his strikeout upside to boost their weekly K totals. An inconsistent command profile creates volatility, but the Ferrets are invested in his upside as a double-digit wins, high-K starter.
579328.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 5.81 ERA/1.58 WHIP, 33 K · currently on D15 IL

592178.name: Kris Bryant
592178.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

592206.name: Nick Castellanos
592206.background: A right-handed hitting outfielder who signed a massive deal with Philadelphia, Nick Castellanos has been one of the most consistent run producers in baseball over a multi-year span. His best work came in Cincinnati and Detroit, where he hit .300+ with 20+ HR. He has declined from his peak but still offers solid counting stat production from a deep Phillies lineup.

592332.name: Kevin Gausman
592332.background: A veteran ace for the Toronto Blue Jays who reinvented himself around an elite split-finger fastball, Kevin Gausman generates elite strikeout rates, exceptional weak contact, and sub-3.50 ERA production that ranks him among the NL's best starters when fully healthy. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets drafted him as a rotation anchor, counting on his plus splitter and sustained excellence to anchor their pitching staff.

592626.name: Joc Pederson
592626.background: Joc Pederson is a left-handed power bat and veteran presence who has found a home in Texas after postseason heroics in San Francisco. He consistently crushes right-handed pitching and has a career-long track record of hitting 20-plus home runs with strong OBP. He's a solid platoon piece and legitimate HR contributor even into his mid-30s, though he needs to play against righties to maximize value.

592662.name: Robbie Ray
592662.background: A former AL Cy Young winner on the Bristol Banshees rotation, Robbie Ray won the 2021 award with Toronto by posting a 2.84 ERA and 248 strikeouts — one of the great single-season pitching performances of the decade. He's battled injuries and inconsistency since but remains a power left-hander whose fastball-slider combination can be elite when his mechanics are right. The Banshees have committed to him as a rotation anchor, and a full healthy season from Ray would dramatically elevate Bristol's CBA pitching profile.

592743.name: Joc Pederson
592743.background: A left-handed hitting outfielder with a long track record of mashing right-handed pitching, Joc Pederson is a platoon threat who has produced 20+ HR in multiple seasons with some of the best home run rates in the league against righties. His defensive value is minimal but his power against right-handers makes him valuable in the right hitting environment.
592743.recentAnalysis: Pederson is off to a slow start with .230/.347/.325 and just 1 HR through 75 PA — the power has been absent early and he's currently not living up to his reputation. A regression to the mean toward more home runs is expected.

592858.name: Rowan Wick
592858.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

592885.name: Christian Yelich
592885.background: A cornerstone of the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls outfield, Christian Yelich won the 2018 NL MVP with one of the most dominant offensive seasons in Brewers history — .326/36 HR/22 SB — and remains a .280-caliber hitter with 20-homer upside in Milwaukee's lineup. Injuries and some mechanical adjustments reduced his production in subsequent years, but the Pepperoni Rolls have held his rights banking on a return toward that elite form. When healthy, there are few more dangerous outfielders on any CBA roster.

593428.name: Xander Bogaerts
593428.background: A cornerstone of the Portland Chinook infield, Xander Bogaerts is a two-time World Series champion and perennial All-Star who built his reputation as one of the most consistent offensive shortstops in baseball over a decade in Boston before signing a massive deal with San Diego. His elite contact skills, gap power, and on-base ability have consistently produced .280-plus averages with 20-plus homers, and Portland has benefited from his steady production as a middle-infield anchor for multiple CBA seasons.

593871.name: Jorge Polanco
593871.background: A free agent second baseman in CBA with solid power and batting average history, Jorge Polanco has been one of the more consistent offensive second basemen in the AL during his Minnesota tenure — posting .270-plus averages with 20-plus homers in multiple seasons. Available for any CBA team looking for second base depth, he profiles as a steady veteran bat with 18-HR upside and useful plate discipline. His injury history is a concern but his floor production is well-established.
593871.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .179/.532 OPS, 1 HR, 2 RBI · currently on D10 IL

595014.name: Blake Treinen
595014.background: A veteran power reliever for the Dodgers, Blake Treinen was one of the most dominant setup arms in the AL during his Oakland peak — featuring a plus sinker that generated elite groundball rates and strikeouts in the most crucial situations. Available in CBA, he profiles as a high-leverage reliever whose elite sinker and plus slider give him strong ratios potential, though his role security depends on Dodgers bullpen deployment.

595879.name: Javier Baez
595879.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .256/.677 OPS, 2 HR, 6 RBI · currently on D10 IL

596019.name: Francisco Lindor
596019.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .226/.669 OPS, 2 HR, 5 RBI · currently on D10 IL

596112.name: Robert Stephenson
596112.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

596115.name: Trevor Story
596115.background: One of the most dangerous shortstops in baseball during his Colorado peak, Trevor Story combined 30-plus homers with 25-plus steals in a five-category profile that made him a perennial top-10 fantasy asset — and has been working to recapture that form in Boston after significant injury interruptions. Available in CBA, he remains a high-ceiling gamble whose full five-category upside is legitimate if he can stay healthy and return to his pre-injury form.
596115.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .206/.547 OPS, 3 HR, 19 RBI · currently on D10 IL

596129.name: Dominic Smith
596129.background: A left-handed hitting first baseman with a pure stroke, Dominic Smith has bounced around several organizations after showing promise early in his career with the Mets. His calling card is quality contact and a good eye at the plate; his liability is limited power and a career marked by inconsistent playing time. He's a useful bench bat when healthy and starting.
596129.recentAnalysis: Smith is having a very strong early 2026, hitting .349/.382/.562 with 4 HR through 68 PA — the batting average and power have both shown up early and he's worth starting wherever he's in the lineup.

601713.name: Nick Pivetta
601713.background: Nick Pivetta is a right-handed starter for the Padres whose mid-90s fastball and devastating curveball give him legitimate strikeout upside when he's commanding his stuff, though his tendency for home run balls and occasional walks keep his fantasy ceiling in check. The Manhattan Mega Rats have him as a rotation piece alongside Yamamoto and Suarez, counting on his strikeout ability to contribute K totals even in starts where he's not at his sharpest. Pivetta's raw stuff makes him a fantasy-relevant arm on his best days.
601713.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 4.50 ERA/1.13 WHIP, 24 K · currently on D15 IL

602104.name: Ramon Urias
602104.background: Ramon Urias is a veteran utility infielder for Baltimore who contributes primarily through his defensive versatility and professional approach at the plate. He has shown occasional pop and a solid walk rate, and his ability to play multiple infield positions makes him useful in fantasy formats that reward positional flexibility. He's primarily a depth piece.
602104.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .158/.595 OPS, 2 HR, 5 RBI · currently on D10 IL

605137.name: Josh Bell
605137.background: A switch-hitting first baseman who hit 37 HR for Pittsburgh in 2019, Josh Bell has bounced around multiple organizations without recapturing that peak but remains a useful power-on-base contributor. With Washington he gets regular at-bats and can contribute 20 HR and a solid walk rate in favorable conditions. A middle-tier first base option.

605141.name: Mookie Betts
605141.background: Mookie Betts is a former MVP and one of the most complete baseball players of his generation, a Dodger outfielder whose combination of hitting, fielding, baserunning, and clutch production have made him a perennial all-star and top-five fantasy asset. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms secured him as a premium keeper alongside Sale and Arraez, knowing that when Mookie is healthy and locked in he's a matchup-winning machine across all five categories. His floor is as high as anyone in the CBA.

605280.name: Clay Holmes
605280.background: A former elite closer for the Yankees who has transitioned to starting for the New York Mets, Clay Holmes built his save reputation on a near-historic sinker that generated one of the highest groundball rates ever measured — and is now translating that elite contact-management ability to a rotation role. The Portland Chinook drafted him as a rotation depth piece with upside, betting on his groundball arsenal to remain effective in an expanded starting role.

605397.name: Joe Musgrove
605397.background: A veteran rotation piece for the Warren Whistlepigs, Joe Musgrove has been one of the most reliable starters in the NL during his San Diego tenure — a crafty right-hander with elite command and a full arsenal of secondary pitches who has delivered multiple sub-3.50 ERA seasons including a no-hitter. The Whistlepigs have relied on his durability and consistency to anchor their rotation, though his injury history has created some anxiety. When healthy, he's a dependable fantasy SP who rarely has blow-up outings.
605397.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

605400.name: Aaron Nola
605400.background: One of the most consistent starting pitchers in baseball over the past decade, Aaron Nola has anchored the Phillies' rotation through multiple postseason runs — delivering reliable sub-3.50 ERA seasons with elite strikeout rates, exceptional command, and the durability to eat 200-plus innings annually. Available in CBA, he profiles as a safe SP2/SP3 whose floor as a strikeout, ERA, and WHIP contributor makes him one of the most reliable starting pitchers on the market.

605483.name: Blake Snell
605483.background: A two-time Cy Young Award winner (2018 AL, 2023 NL) with some of the nastiest raw stuff in the sport, Blake Snell is a high-strikeout lefty who signed with the Dodgers after his career year with San Diego. His elite K rate (12+ per 9) is countered by high walk totals and a history of abbreviated outings, but when locked in he is a fantasy ace. Durability and control remain the perennial concerns.
605483.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 12.00 ERA/2.67 WHIP, 5 K · currently on D15 IL

605488.name: Jeffrey Springs
605488.background: A crafty left-handed starter who has rejuvenated his career for the Bristol Banshees, Jeffrey Springs transformed himself from a bullpen piece into one of the better rotation bargains in the AL with Tampa Bay, relying on deception, plus changeup tunneling, and elite command to get outs with well-below-average velocity. His Tommy John surgery cost him 2024, but Bristol held his rights knowing a healthy Springs is a legitimate fantasy SP with elite WHIP and ERA potential. The Banshees are reaping the rewards of that patient investment.

605540.name: Brandon Woodruff
605540.background: One of the most talented starters in baseball when healthy, Brandon Woodruff is a Brewers right-hander with a mid-90s fastball and devastating slider who has dealt with significant shoulder injuries but remains a top-15 starter when right. Available in CBA, he profiles as a high-ceiling SP1 gamble whose elite stuff makes him worth rostering in any format where you can absorb the injury risk.
605540.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 3.60 ERA/1.03 WHIP, 25 K · currently on D15 IL

606192.name: Teoscar Hernandez
606192.background: A power bat in the Delmarva Emus outfield, Teoscar Hernandez has developed into one of the more dangerous right-handed hitters in the NL, combining 30-plus homer upside with a high-energy approach that generates big run-scoring opportunities. His time with Toronto, Seattle, and now the Dodgers has demonstrated consistent power production in any environment, and the Emus are benefiting from his placement in one of baseball's best lineups. In a stacked Delmarva roster, Hernandez provides the power punch that completes their outfield.

607043.name: Brandon Nimmo
607043.background: A key piece of the Delmarva Emus outfield, Brandon Nimmo is one of baseball's elite on-base presences — a leadoff hitter whose extraordinary patience and walk rates produce OBP figures consistently above .380. His re-signing with the Mets confirmed his status as a franchise anchor, and he provides solid counting stat accumulation from the top of New York's order with 20-homer upside as a bonus. The Emus rely on his runs, walks, and reliability to help power one of CBA's more stacked rosters.

607074.name: Carlos Rodon
607074.background: A high-upside starter on the Portland Chinook rotation, Carlos Rodon's career trajectory has been defined by electric stuff and persistent injury — a 2021 near no-hitter and a 2022 All-Star campaign serving as peaks in between multiple shoulder and elbow procedures. When healthy, his fastball-slider combination is among the most dominant in the AL, and Portland has held his rights through yet another IL stint knowing a full season of Rodon is a legitimate fantasy ace. The Chinook are waiting on the healthy version they know exists.

607192.name: Tyler Glasnow
607192.background: Tyler Glasnow is one of the most devastating pitchers in baseball when healthy, a Dodger ace whose 100-mph fastball and elite curveball combination gives him one of the highest K/9 rates in the game when he's on the mound. The Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets have him as their rotation centerpiece alongside Logan Webb, and when Glasnow is fully locked in he's a matchup-winning ace capable of shutting down any lineup. His strikeout ceiling is among the highest of any pitcher available to the CBA.
607192.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 2.72 ERA/0.83 WHIP, 49 K · currently on D15 IL

607208.name: Trea Turner
607208.background: Trea Turner is one of the fastest players in baseball, a perennial 30-steal shortstop for the Phillies who combines his elite speed with consistent power and a .280+ average to deliver across all five fantasy categories. The Portland Chinook landed him as their most dynamic offensive weapon, pairing him with Daylen Lile in an outfield/infield combo that gives the Chinook premium speed at the top of their lineup. Turner's track record as a multi-category producer makes him one of the most reliable keepers in the league.

607625.name: Seth Lugo
607625.background: A veteran rotation anchor for the Delmarva Emus, Seth Lugo reinvented himself from a high-leverage reliever into a legitimate starting pitcher with Kansas City — posting a sub-2.00 ERA in his debut starting season with elite command and an excellent cutter-sinker combination. The Emus secured him as a reliable mid-rotation piece knowing his floor as a keeper is among the safest in CBA. Delmarva relies on his durability and consistent ratios to anchor their staff alongside Bradly and the younger arms they've accumulated.

608032.name: Carlos Estevez
608032.background: A high-leverage closer with a premium fastball who has accumulated saves for the Angels, Phillies, and Royals, Carlos Estevez combines plus velocity with a sharp breaking ball to generate strikeouts and hold ninth-inning leads. Available in CBA, he profiles as a save-accumulation arm whose stuff is legitimate but whose role security depends on whichever team gives him a closing opportunity.
608032.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 162.00 ERA/18.00 WHIP, 0 K · currently on D15 IL

608324.name: Alex Bregman
608324.background: Alex Bregman is a two-time World Series champion and one of the smartest hitters in baseball, a disciplined third baseman now with the Red Sox whose elite plate patience and consistent power have made him a fantasy cornerstone for years. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him in the middle of their lineup alongside Judge and Turang, counting on his on-base skills and 25-HR pop to provide steady run production. Bregman's walk rate and baseball IQ make him a floor-raising asset on any roster.

608331.name: Max Fried
608331.background: Max Fried is one of the best left-handed starters in baseball, a crafty Yankee whose elite command, plus curveball, and deceptive delivery allow him to dominate lineups without overpowering them with pure velocity. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys have him anchoring their pitching staff alongside Zack Wheeler, and when Fried is dealing the Space Cowboys have a genuine top-of-the-rotation weapon. His ability to miss bats while also generating weak contact makes him effective in any ballpark.
608331.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 3.21 ERA/1.01 WHIP, 50 K · currently on D15 IL

608348.name: Carson Kelly
608348.background: The veteran catcher for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Carson Kelly is a dependable two-way backstop who provides solid defense and a patient, contact-oriented offensive approach that produces useful batting averages and walk rates. The Sky Chiefs rely on his professionalism and consistency at a position that can be volatile week to week in fantasy, and his ability to post .280-plus averages while providing adequate power makes him a stable floor at the C slot. He pairs well with Alejandro Kirk to give Syracuse genuine depth behind the plate.

608369.name: Corey Seager
608369.background: Corey Seager is a two-time World Series MVP and one of the most dangerous left-handed hitters in baseball, a cornerstone shortstop for the Rangers who combines plus power with excellent on-base skills. The Delmarva Emus pair him with Ketel Marte and Ohtani as part of a formidable offensive core, giving them three premium bats to anchor every week. When healthy, Seager is one of the most productive shortstops in all of fantasy baseball.
608369.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .179/.639 OPS, 7 HR, 20 RBI · currently on D10 IL

608379.name: Michael Wacha
608379.background: A reliable innings-eater and rotation piece for the Warren Whistlepigs, Michael Wacha has rebuilt his career as a contact-management specialist who generates weak contact and consistently posts ERA figures well below his stuff would suggest. The Whistlepigs have relied on his ability to go deep into games and protect their bullpen, and his durability across multiple seasons has been one of their rotation's quiet competitive advantages. Warren values his floor production even if the ceiling is modest.

608577.name: Zack Littell
608577.background: A right-handed starter for Tampa Bay who converted from relief with mixed results, Zack Littell relies on a deceptive delivery and a varied pitch mix rather than overpowering stuff. His strikeout rate is modest and he's prone to stretches of poor command, making him a risky fantasy option even with regular starts.
608577.recentAnalysis: Littell is struggling significantly in 2026 with a 7.56 ERA and 1.68 WHIP across 25 IP — he's been one of the worst pitchers in baseball by ERA and is not currently startable in any format.

608701.name: Rob Refsnyder
608701.background: Rob Refsnyder is a journeyman outfielder who carved out a roster role with Boston primarily through his versatility and ability to produce in platoon situations, particularly against left-handed pitching. He's never been a full-time player but his professional approach and solid OBP give him value in deep formats when playing time is guaranteed. His power has been a pleasant surprise at times but he's more of a fantasy streamer than a keeper-caliber asset.

609280.name: Miguel Andujar
609280.background: A former Yankees top prospect who finished second in AL Rookie of the Year voting in 2018, Miguel Andujar has reinvented himself as a contact-oriented DH/corner infielder now with the Athletics after years of injuries derailed his original trajectory. Available in CBA, he profiles as a .280-hitting bat with modest power whose best value comes as a batting average contributor with useful RBI depth.

621020.name: Dansby Swanson
621020.background: Dansby Swanson is a veteran shortstop for the Cubs whose above-average bat, solid power, and Gold Glove defense have made him one of the NL's most well-rounded middle infielders since arriving in Chicago. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys have him as additional middle infield depth alongside Cruz and Lindor, giving them quality production across their infield even when injuries or rests arise. Swanson's consistent approach and leadership give the Space Cowboys a reliable veteran presence.

621043.name: Carlos Correa
621043.background: A franchise-level acquisition for the Delmarva Emus, Carlos Correa is a former World Series champion and ALCS MVP who has maintained his status as one of the best offensive shortstops in baseball through consistent 25-homer, 75-RBI seasons with elite on-base skills. His patience at the plate, postseason excellence, and defensive reputation have made him a cornerstone wherever he plays. The Emus count on his steady middle-of-the-lineup production to anchor their CBA roster year after year.
621043.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .279/.787 OPS, 3 HR, 16 RBI · currently on D10 IL

621053.name: Tyler Ferguson
621053.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 27.00 ERA/3.00 WHIP, 1 K · currently on RM IL

621242.name: Edwin Diaz
621242.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 10.50 ERA/2.33 WHIP, 10 K · currently on D60 IL

621244.name: Jose Berrios
621244.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

621381.name: Matt Strahm
621381.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 3.86 ERA/1.29 WHIP, 17 K · currently on D15 IL

621438.name: Tyrone Taylor
621438.background: Tyrone Taylor is a versatile outfielder who has earned playing time with multiple teams through his solid defense and contact-oriented approach. He has some power and contributes in multiple counting categories when given regular playing time. His fantasy value is dependent on lineup security, but he's a useful utility outfielder in deeper formats.
621438.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .186/.530 OPS, 3 HR, 14 RBI · currently on D10 IL

621439.name: Byron Buxton
621439.background: Byron Buxton is the most gifted all-around player in baseball when healthy, a center fielder for the Twins whose elite speed, power, and defense make him a unicorn who can win fantasy matchups on his own during hot streaks. The Portland Chinook kept him as a high-ceiling risk-reward play, knowing that even a half-season of Buxton production makes him worth the roster spot. When he's locked in, there's no more exciting player to watch in the CBA.

621493.name: Taylor Ward
621493.background: A patient, high-OBP bat for the Portland Chinook outfield, Taylor Ward emerged as a late-blooming hitter who maximized his power and on-base skills after changing his approach at the plate. His ability to work counts and draw walks at an above-average rate gives him a strong OBP profile, and he's shown legitimate 20-homer upside in his best seasons. Portland values him as a reliable floor play who won't hurt you in any category and contributes in runs and walks.

622534.name: Alek Thomas
622534.background: A speedy center fielder for Arizona who plays above-average defense and offers a useful combination of batting average and steals, Alek Thomas is the kind of player who contributes across multiple categories without dominating any of them. He has legitimate 15-HR, 20-SB upside from the top of a strong Diamondbacks lineup and can be a solid multi-category contributor.
622534.recentAnalysis: Thomas is off to a decent start with .213/.313/.340 and 2 HR and 4 SB through 80 PA — the speed is showing early and even if the average stays around .213 the stolen bases and run-scoring potential keep him viable.

622554.name: Seranthony Dominguez
622554.background: A power reliever and save contributor for the Manhattan Mega Rats, Seranthony Dominguez features elite velocity and a plus slider that have made him one of Philadelphia's most dangerous late-game options. The Mega Rats acquired his rights knowing his closer history and strikeout ability translate directly to fantasy value, and his saves and holds have been a meaningful part of Manhattan's weekly strategy. His electric stuff gives him the ceiling of a top-10 fantasy closer when he's fully healthy and in command.

622761.name: Jorge Mateo
622761.background: Jorge Mateo is a speed demon infielder who relies almost entirely on his blazing baserunning ability for fantasy value. His stolen base rate is elite when he plays regularly — he's been one of the top stolen base contributors in baseball — but his light bat and low on-base percentage make him a drag in batting average categories. He's a pure SB contributor who needs a lineup spot to be relevant.

623149.name: Paul Sewald
623149.background: The closer for the Delmarva Emus and one of the most reliable save sources in the AL, Paul Sewald has posted dominant save totals for Seattle with a plus fastball and a changeup that generates elite whiff rates in the highest-leverage situations. The Emus have committed significant roster value to him knowing his save conversion ability is among the best in baseball, and his presence in the back of Delmarva's bullpen gives them a weekly CBA edge. He and Helsley give the Emus the most formidable late-game combination in the league.

623352.name: Josh Hader
623352.background: The premier closer on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster and one of the most dominant relief pitchers of his generation, Josh Hader has converted saves at an elite rate throughout his career with a fastball-slider combination that renders lineups helpless. His ability to generate strikeouts in the ninth inning while protecting slim leads has made him a fantasy closer royalty, and the Ferrets have leaned on his save production as a weekly CBA advantage. Even into his 30s, Hader's stuff remains among the best in any bullpen.
623352.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

623465.name: Evan Phillips
623465.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

623474.name: Jimmy Herget
623474.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 5.06 ERA/1.38 WHIP, 17 K · currently on D15 IL

623993.name: Anthony Santander
623993.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

624133.name: Ranger Suarez
624133.background: Ranger Suarez is a left-handed starter for the Phillies who has quietly established himself as one of the more effective mid-rotation arms in the NL, relying on excellent sink and arm-side run to generate ground balls and limit walks in his typically efficient outings. The Manhattan Mega Rats have him in their rotation alongside Yamamoto and Crochet, giving them a dependable innings-eater who can hold his own in most matchups. Suarez's consistency and ability to work deep into games make him a reliable fantasy SP3.

624431.name: Jose Trevino
624431.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .138/.339 OPS, 0 HR, 2 RBI · currently on D10 IL

624585.name: Jorge Soler
624585.background: A veteran power bat best remembered for his walk-off home run in the 2021 World Series, Jorge Soler has delivered multiple 30-plus HR seasons that made him one of the most dangerous right-handed power bats in fantasy baseball — combining elite pull-side power with enough contact to hit .240 and drive in 90-plus runs when healthy. Available in CBA with the Angels, he profiles as a 30-HR DH/outfielder whose power ceiling remains among the best available at his cost.

624641.name: Edmundo Sosa
624641.background: A free agent utility infielder in CBA with multi-position flexibility, Edmundo Sosa has carved out a dependable bench role through his ability to play every infield position and contribute useful batting average in part-time situations. Available for any CBA team in need of infield depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting utility player whose positional flexibility gives rosters valuable insurance. A safe, low-variance depth addition.

625643.name: Reynaldo Lopez
625643.background: A hard-throwing right-hander for the Braves who transformed himself from reliever to legitimate rotation piece with an improved pitch mix and much better command, Reynaldo Lopez generates strikeouts with a mid-90s fastball and developing secondary arsenal. Available in CBA, he profiles as a mid-rotation arm with solid strikeout upside and improving ratios as he continues to refine his command in a full starting role.

628406.name: Ha-seong Kim
628406.background: A slick-fielding shortstop from Korea, Ha-seong Kim established himself as a multi-position defender with surprising power and above-average contact for San Diego. He suffered a shoulder injury that cost him significant time and was one of the notable 2025 IL casualties. When healthy he's a solid middle-infield option with 15+ HR and 15+ SB potential.
628406.recentAnalysis: Ha-seong Kim has not yet appeared in 2026 — he is likely working back from the shoulder injury and his return timeline is unclear. Do not roster until he's confirmed healthy and in the lineup.

628451.name: Andy Ibanez
628451.background: Andy Ibanez is a veteran utility infielder and outfielder who provides positional flexibility and a professional approach at the plate. He's shown the ability to contribute in limited duty, particularly with gap power at times, but his overall profile is that of a bench player rather than an everyday contributor. His fantasy value is depth-oriented.

628452.name: Raisel Iglesias
628452.background: The closer for the Manhattan Mega Rats and one of the most reliable save sources in the AL, Raisel Iglesias features elite late-game stuff with a mid-90s fastball and a devastating curveball that generates strikeouts in the highest-pressure situations. The Mega Rats have built their bullpen around his ability to close out leads consistently, and his save conversion rate has been one of the best in baseball for multiple seasons. Manhattan views his save production as a weekly CBA competitive advantage.

630105.name: Jake Cronenworth
630105.background: Jake Cronenworth is a highly versatile infielder who played an enormous role for San Diego at his peak, contributing across multiple positions while posting solid OBP and surprising power for his profile. He's been a reliable 15-HR, .270 type hitter and his positional flexibility makes him useful in fantasy leagues that reward multi-position eligibility. He missed significant time in 2025 with injury and his 2026 projections reflect some uncertainty.
630105.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .144/.468 OPS, 1 HR, 4 RBI · currently on D7 IL

640451.name: Hunter Harvey
640451.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 6.75 ERA/1.50 WHIP, 4 K · currently on D60 IL

641154.name: Pablo Lopez
641154.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

641343.name: Jake Bauers
641343.background: A versatile bat on the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls roster, Jake Bauers brings a well-rounded offensive profile with surprising power upside and solid plate discipline that makes him a useful contributor in CBA's multi-category scoring. His ability to play first base and outfield gives the Pepperoni Rolls positional flexibility, and his power-speed combination at the plate profiles toward 20 HR and double-digit steals when everything is working. West Virginia has valued him as a depth piece with upside.

641487.name: J.P. Crawford
641487.background: J.P. Crawford is a solid defensive shortstop for Seattle who has carved out a steady career as a high-OBP, low-power contributor. He draws walks at an above-average rate and provides a reliable batting average floor, though his lack of power limits his fantasy ceiling in most formats. He's a steady but unspectacular option who provides stability without upside.

641584.name: Jake Fraley
641584.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with solid plate discipline and gap power, Jake Fraley has produced useful batting average and on-base numbers during his stints with Tampa Bay and Cincinnati — featuring a patient approach and the ability to hit to all fields. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting OF with modest power and a disciplined approach that generates walks. His best value comes in on-base and run-scoring categories.
641584.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .232/.690 OPS, 2 HR, 5 RBI · currently on D10 IL

641729.name: Joe Jimenez
641729.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

641778.name: Eric Lauer
641778.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with useful upside, Eric Lauer is a crafty left-hander whose command and secondary stuff have made him a reliable mid-rotation option during his Milwaukee and New York stints. Available for any CBA team to claim, he provides a solid floor as an innings-eater with modest K upside and good WHIP potential. He projects best as a rotation piece for a team looking to add depth rather than a frontline ace, but his consistency gives him value as a streaming or back-end option.

641793.name: Zack Littell
641793.background: Zack Littell has bounced around several organizations before landing a rotation spot with Tampa Bay, where his ability to command multiple pitches and generate weak contact earned him a chance to start. He's a finesse-heavy pitcher who won't overpower anyone but can be effective in a groundball-inducing system. His fantasy ceiling is limited but he provides ratio stability as a streaming option.

641816.name: Tyler Mahle
641816.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with high strikeout upside, Tyler Mahle features a premium four-seamer and excellent slider that have produced some of the best K/9 rates in the NL when he's healthy. His career has been defined by persistent injuries but the stuff has never been in question — a healthy Mahle is a genuine fantasy SP2. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a high-variance, high-ceiling add for teams willing to gamble on his health.

641933.name: Tyler O'Neill
641933.background: Tyler O'Neill is a power-first outfielder whose elite raw power and high strikeout rate create a volatile but high-ceiling fantasy profile. He's been one of the most injury-prone players in baseball over his career, limiting his counting stat totals, but when healthy he's capable of 30-homer campaigns with strong RBI and run contributions. He landed with Baltimore after leaving St. Louis and is a pure boom-or-bust asset.

641941.name: Emilio Pagan
641941.background: The closer for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets and a veteran save accumulator, Emilio Pagan has built a late-career closer role with a deceptive delivery and plus slider that generate strikeouts in the ninth inning despite below-elite velocity. The Ferrets have relied on his save production as a consistent CBA advantage, and his ability to convert leads in high-pressure situations has proven more reliable than his modest stuff might suggest. Ft. Meade's bullpen strategy is built around his closer role.
641941.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 6.43 ERA/1.43 WHIP, 11 K · currently on D15 IL

642086.name: Dominic Smith
642086.background: Dominic Smith is a left-handed first baseman who has battled through inconsistency and health issues to remain a productive hitter when given opportunities. He has solid power and a good approach against right-handed pitching, making him a useful platoon bat. His 2026 campaign with San Francisco has given him a chance to re-establish value after years of limited playing time.

642121.name: Cole Sulser
642121.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 3.68 ERA/1.36 WHIP, 16 K · currently on D15 IL

642201.name: Eli White
642201.background: A free agent utility outfielder in CBA with speed and positional flexibility, Eli White has contributed as a bench piece and occasional starter with Texas and Chicago — bringing stolen base ability and multi-position coverage to any roster. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth with speed upside, he profiles as a .250-hitting utility OF with 15-SB potential when given regular opportunities.

642207.name: Devin Williams
642207.background: The closer for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets and the owner of one of the most unhittable pitches in baseball, Devin Williams' 'Airbender' changeup generates the highest whiff rate of any pitch thrown regularly in the majors — a sinker-screwball hybrid that seemingly defies physics. Injuries have created some drama in his closer timeline, but the Ferrets have committed to him as their primary save source knowing the ceiling is historically dominant. When healthy, he's the most swing-and-miss closer in all of CBA.

642215.name: Weston Wilson
642215.background: Weston Wilson is a journeyman utility infielder who earned a roster spot with Philadelphia through his versatility and solid contact in limited opportunities. He doesn't have a standout tool but contributes across multiple categories in small samples and can fill multiple positions in daily fantasy or utility slots. His value is entirely contingent on playing time.

642397.name: Gregory Soto
642397.background: A hard-throwing setup reliever for the Bristol Banshees, Gregory Soto features triple-digit velocity and a slider that generates strikeouts in the highest-leverage situations for Philadelphia's bullpen. The Banshees have used him to accumulate holds and bolster their late-game depth, and his pure stuff makes him one of the more dangerous arms they carry. Bristol has built one of the most formidable late-game combinations in CBA with Soto, Chapman, and Fairbanks.

642585.name: Felix Bautista
642585.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

642701.name: Dennis Santana
642701.background: A hard-throwing setup arm for the Pirates who has built a career on elite velocity and plus secondary stuff, Dennis Santana is a useful high-leverage reliever who generates strikeouts and can handle multiple innings in bullpen configurations. Available in CBA, he profiles as a ratios-friendly reliever whose velocity and wipeout slider give him strong WHIP and K/9 upside in any bullpen role.

642708.name: Amed Rosario
642708.background: A versatile shortstop and outfielder with a long MLB resume including stints with Cleveland and the Dodgers, Amed Rosario is a contact-first hitter whose ability to play multiple positions and hit for a .265+ average gives him value as a depth option when he finds a roster spot. Available in CBA as a free agent, he profiles as a gap-to-gap hitter with multi-position eligibility and modest upside whose fantasy value depends on landing regular playing time.

642715.name: Willy Adames
642715.background: A power-hitting shortstop and one of the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls' middle-infield anchors, Willy Adames has consistently produced 20-plus homers from the position across Tampa Bay, Milwaukee, and now San Francisco. His willingness to walk combined with 25-homer potential makes him one of the better offensive shortstops in the NL, and the Pepperoni Rolls have benefited from his reliable production at a position of strength. His new Giants ballpark has proven home run-friendly for right-handed hitters.

642786.name: Ramon Urias
642786.background: A versatile utility infielder for Baltimore who provides multi-position eligibility and solid contact skills, Ramon Urias is more valuable for his defensive flexibility than his offensive production. He can contribute a reasonable batting average in limited at-bats but lacks the power or speed to be a fantasy starter in standard formats.
642786.recentAnalysis: Urias is hitting .180/.322/.376 with 2 HR through 59 PA — the walks and power are present but the batting average drag makes him hard to start in anything but the deepest formats.

643217.name: Andrew Benintendi
643217.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with strong contact skills, Andrew Benintendi has been a consistent .280-plus hitter throughout his career with excellent gap power and professional at-bats in multiple AL lineups. Available for any CBA team looking for a reliable contact bat, he profiles as a .275-hitting OF with 15-HR upside and consistent on-base production. His contact approach and durability make him a safe floor addition.

643289.name: Mauricio Dubon
643289.background: A reliable utility contributor for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Mauricio Dubon has built a long career on consistent contact, multi-position eligibility, and smart baserunning that helps teams in counting stats without being a liability anywhere. He plays outfield and infield equally well and brings solid batting average to the Fuzzy Bottoms' lineup from a spot where many teams play defensive liabilities. North Georgia values his flexibility and reliability as a veteran contributor who keeps their roster construction options open.

643396.name: Isiah Kiner-Falefa
643396.background: Isiah Kiner-Falefa is a veteran utility infielder who has played for multiple teams and carved out a career through his ability to play multiple positions adequately. He's a contact-first hitter with modest power who contributes stolen bases from time to time, but his overall offensive profile is below average. His fantasy value comes entirely from multi-position eligibility and stolen bases.

643446.name: Jeff McNeil
643446.background: A former NL batting champion for the Mets who brings one of the best pure contact profiles in the NL — a slap-and-gap approach with exceptional strikeout avoidance that powered his .326 batting title season — Jeff McNeil has battled injuries since but remains a high-floor batting average asset when healthy. Available in CBA, he profiles as a .280-hitting second baseman whose elite contact skills and positional flexibility make him a useful batting average contributor.

645261.name: Sandy Alcantara
645261.background: The workhorse ace of the Syracuse Sky Chiefs rotation and a former NL Cy Young winner, Sandy Alcantara is known for throwing the most innings of any pitcher in baseball — routinely completing 7-8 innings on elite pitch efficiency. Tommy John surgery cost him 2024-25, but the Sky Chiefs held his rights knowing that when healthy, his combination of innings, groundballs, and elite durability is unmatched in CBA. Syracuse needs him to be their anchor, and his return to the rotation has been one of their most important storylines.

645277.name: Ozzie Albies
645277.background: Ozzie Albies is a dynamic second baseman for the Braves who combines elite athleticism with surprising power, consistently posting 25+ home runs while adding stolen base contributions from the top of Atlanta's lineup. The Portland Chinook have him as a key piece of their infield alongside Paredes and Turner, counting on his power-speed combo to produce multi-category value week after week. Albies' combination of speed, pop, and a Gold Glove-caliber glove make him one of the most exciting second basemen in fantasy baseball.

645302.name: Victor Robles
645302.background: Once a highly-touted top prospect for Washington, Victor Robles never fully developed his bat and was eventually traded to Seattle. He's a plus defender in center field with elite speed, but his contact rates and power have been consistently underwhelming in the majors. His fantasy value is limited to stolen bases in deep formats.

647304.name: Josh Naylor
647304.background: Josh Naylor is a beloved first baseman for the Guardians who has made himself into one of baseball's most underrated sluggers, a left-handed power bat with elite on-base skills and the kind of clubhouse energy that drives winning. The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms kept him as a core piece of their lineup alongside Chris Sale and Andy Pages, banking on his 30-HR upside and patient approach to produce big run totals. Naylor's gritty approach makes him a fan favorite and a dependable fantasy asset.

650093.name: Luis Rengifo
650093.background: A utility infielder for the Angels with multi-position eligibility, Luis Rengifo is a contact hitter who can play second base, shortstop, or third base. His fantasy appeal is mostly eligibility-based — he contributes a few categories across the board without excelling in any — but in a year with a full-time role he can be a useful piece on thin rosters.
650093.recentAnalysis: Rengifo is struggling with a .173/.224/.279 line through 85 PA — well below his expected output, this is a significant slump that makes him not worth starting until he turns it around.

650391.name: Eloy Jimenez
650391.background: Eloy Jimenez is a powerful left-handed hitter with massive raw power who has been one of the most injury-prone players in baseball over his career. When healthy, he's capable of elite run production and HR totals, with a swing that generates elite exit velocities. The problem has always been that he almost never stays healthy for a full season. He's a high-ceiling, high-risk fantasy asset who should be rostered with injury expectations priced in.

650402.name: Gleyber Torres
650402.background: Gleyber Torres is a middle infielder now with the Tigers who has posted multiple 25-HR seasons with the Yankees and brings consistent power and contact from the second base position. The Warren Whistlepigs have him as a lineup complement alongside Hoerner and Soderstrom, counting on his power from the middle infield and his ability to produce in a loaded Tigers lineup. Torres' consistent extra-base hit production gives the Whistlepigs a reliable run-producing option at second.
650402.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: .259/.717 OPS, 2 HR, 11 RBI · currently on D10 IL

650489.name: Willi Castro
650489.background: Willi Castro is a switch-hitting utility player for Minnesota who brings positional flexibility and a solid contact-speed combination. He's shown the ability to contribute across multiple lineup spots and earn regular playing time as a versatile reserve. His fantasy value is limited by modest power but the steals and batting average keep him viable in deeper leagues.

650490.name: Yandy Diaz
650490.background: Yandy Diaz is one of baseball's most disciplined and contact-oriented hitters, a first baseman for the Rays whose remarkably low strikeout rate and consistently high batting average give him one of the safest fantasy floors of any first base option in the game. The Manhattan Mega Rats have him as a lineup complement to Acuna and Caminero, using his on-base skills to set the table for their power hitters and keep the offense moving. Diaz's elite contact and walk rate make him a floor-raising keeper for the Mega Rats.

650496.name: Willi Castro
650496.background: A utility infielder who provides multi-position eligibility across the infield and occasional outfield, Willi Castro is a contact-oriented switch-hitter who will never be a power source but can contribute batting average and a handful of steals. His versatility keeps him on big league rosters and makes him useful in deeper leagues.
650496.recentAnalysis: Castro is producing at a decent clip with .246/.329/.335 and 1 HR and 2 SB through 76 PA — solid utility production without a standout category. Worth rostering in deep leagues for eligibility.

650556.name: Bryan Abreu
650556.background: A high-leverage setup arm for the Sugar Land Space Cowboys, Bryan Abreu has carved out an indispensable role in Houston's bullpen with a mid-to-upper-90s fastball and a slider that generates elite swing-and-miss rates. The Space Cowboys rely on his ability to bridge from the starter to the closer in high-stakes situations, and his holds and occasional save opportunities contribute meaningfully to their weekly CBA totals. He's the kind of reliable power reliever that championship CBA rosters are built around.

650633.name: Michael King
650633.background: A free agent starting pitcher with strong upside available in the CBA, Michael King converted from one of the Yankees' best relievers into a quality starter for San Diego — immediately posting sub-3.00 ERA figures with excellent command and a diverse pitch mix. Available to any CBA team looking to add a reliable mid-rotation arm, he represents one of the best starting pitcher options in the free agent pool with a high floor and genuine SP2 upside. Any manager who adds him immediately strengthens their rotation.

650644.name: Aaron Civale
650644.background: Aaron Civale is a sinker-heavy right-hander who has bounced between Cleveland and Tampa Bay, building a reputation as a groundball-inducing finesse pitcher. His best seasons showcased excellent walk rates and solid WHIP numbers, relying on location and movement over pure stuff. He's a reliable mid-rotation option when healthy and pitching in good defensive environments, though his modest strikeout rate limits his fantasy ceiling.
650644.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 4.20 ERA/1.47 WHIP, 39 K · currently on D15 IL

650859.name: Luis Rengifo
650859.background: Luis Rengifo is a versatile utility infielder for the Los Angeles Angels who has managed to stay in the lineup by playing multiple positions adequately. He has shown some surprising power at times and contributes modest stolen bases, but his overall offensive profile is inconsistent. He's primarily a deep league option whose value comes from eligibility at 2B, 3B, and SS.

655316.name: Andruw Monasterio
655316.background: Andruw Monasterio is a versatile utility infielder for Milwaukee who earns roster spots through his defensive flexibility and ability to play multiple positions competently. He shows a patient approach at the plate and can contribute across several batting categories in small doses, though his upside is limited by the lack of a standout offensive tool. In daily or deep leagues he's a viable fill-in.

655889.name: Manuel Rodriguez
655889.recentAnalysis: currently on D60 IL — no 2026 stats yet.

656302.name: Dylan Cease
656302.background: The ace of the Manhattan Mega Rats rotation and one of the most electric starters in the AL, Dylan Cease combines a triple-digit fastball with one of the best sliders in baseball to generate elite strikeout rates and regularly post double-digit K outings. His San Diego tenure has made him a perennial top-15 SP in all fantasy formats, and the Mega Rats secured him knowing he provides the kind of ceiling arm that wins CBA championships. When Cease is on, no lineup in the league is comfortable facing him.
656302.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 3.05 ERA/1.21 WHIP, 92 K · currently on D15 IL

656427.name: Jack Flaherty
656427.background: Jack Flaherty is a power right-hander who reached his peak with St. Louis before moving on to Detroit, combining an elite slider with a plus fastball to generate elite strikeout rates. He's been one of the more volatile pitchers in baseball — capable of ace-level starts but prone to bad stretches when his command wavers. His upside is legitimate SP2 value but requires tolerance for ratio variance.

656464.name: Kevin Ginkel
656464.background: A veteran setup reliever for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets, Kevin Ginkel has carved out a consistent role in Arizona's bullpen with a power sinker and reliable command that generates groundballs and keeps high-leverage runners from scoring. The Ferrets have used him as a flexible bullpen piece who can contribute in multiple leverage situations, and his holds production has been a meaningful part of Ft. Meade's weekly strategy. He's the kind of unsung bullpen workhorse that helps CBA teams stay competitive in relief categories.

656537.name: Derek Hill

665926.name: Andres Gimenez

His ability to contribute in multiple offensive categories while playing premium defense has made him a valuable fantasy asset, and Bristol has used his multi-category upside to stay competitive across the board.

665953.name: Andres Chaparro

He's working to establish himself at the major league level and has the physical tools to develop into a 20-plus homer contributor. His upside as a power bat in a developing lineup makes him worth tracking in dynasty formats.

666018.name: Jonathan Aranda

The Manhattan Mega Rats grabbed him as a high-floor lineup complement, pairing him with Acuna and Caminero to create a lineup that produces runs from multiple spots. Aranda's on-base skills and clutch hitting give the Mega Rats a reliable run-producer at a position of value.

His tools are legitimate and he's worth a roster spot in CBA.

666160.name: Mickey Moniak
666160.background: Mickey Moniak is an outfielder for the Rockies whose career has been a long journey from first-overall bust to legitimate power threat, developing a pull-happy approach that plays well in Coors Field. The Warren Whistlepigs took a chance on him as a Coors Field power play, betting that his emerging pop in Colorado's thin air gives them a double-digit home run boost to their already deep lineup. Moniak's raw power and ballpark context make him a high-floor Coors play for the Whistlepigs.

666176.name: Jo Adell
666176.background: Jo Adell is a toolsy outfielder for the Angels whose massive raw power, elite athleticism, and improving contact rate have made him one of baseball's most tantalizing boom-or-bust talents as he continues to develop. The Delmarva Emus have him rounding out their deep outfield lineup, betting on his physical gifts and emerging plate discipline to finally unlock his long-anticipated power numbers in a meaningful way. When Adell gets hot, he's one of the most dangerous hitters the Emus have on the roster.

666181.name: Will Benson
666181.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with power upside and speed, Will Benson has shown the raw tools to be a useful contributor — featuring the kind of bat speed and athleticism that generated attention as a prospect. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth with raw power potential, he profiles as a .250-hitting OF with 15-HR and 10-SB ceiling if given regular opportunities.

666182.name: Bo Bichette
666182.background: Bo Bichette is a high-energy shortstop for the Blue Jays who combines excellent contact skills with surprising power and stolen base ability, giving him legitimate five-category upside when locked in. The Bristol Banshees have him as a middle infield depth piece alongside Henderson and Gunnar, counting on his consistent contact to provide a steady floor of hits and runs. Bichette's aggressive approach and line-drive stroke make him a reliable producer even in down stretches.

666200.name: Jesus Luzardo
666200.background: Jesus Luzardo is a left-handed starter for the Phillies with a dominant three-pitch arsenal headlined by a plus fastball-changeup combination that when healthy makes him a legitimate top-of-the-rotation arm. The West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls have him as a potential ace-level talent in their rotation, and when Luzardo is clicking with his full arsenal he's one of the most dominant pitchers the Pepperoni Rolls can throw out there. His upside on his best days rivals any SP in the CBA.

666305.name: Miguel Amaya
666305.background: A catching prospect for the Cubs who was a key part of their rebuild, Miguel Amaya is a switch-hitter with solid power and an advanced approach behind the plate. He profiles as a 15-HR catcher with good on-base skills, which at a position this thin makes him a legitimate mid-tier fantasy catcher.
666305.recentAnalysis: Amaya is off to a solid start with .234/.364/.383 and 2 HR through 55 PA — the walks are excellent for a catcher and the power is beginning to emerge. A viable streaming catcher with upside.

Available in CBA, he profiles as a boom-or-bust 20-HR bat who projects best against left-handed pitching and offers useful power in a corner outfield spot.

He provides a decent average base and contributes some stolen bases, making him a useful depth piece in deeper leagues. His path to consistent playing time will determine his fantasy ceiling.

667755.name: Jose Soriano
667755.background: The ace of the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets rotation and one of the most electrifying young starters in the AL, Jose Soriano features a triple-digit fastball and a devastating breaking ball that generate elite swing-and-miss rates throughout the lineup. Long viewed as one of the Angels' most exciting pitching prospects, he has emerged into a full-fledged fantasy ace whose pure stuff is as good as anyone in the game. The Ferrets struck gold keeping Soriano, and he's rapidly establishing himself as one of the most dominant pitchers in all of CBA.

668227.name: Randy Arozarena
668227.background: A popular piece of the Portland Chinook outfield, Randy Arozarena became a folk hero during the 2020 postseason with a record-setting performance, then validated that debut with back-to-back 20-HR, 20-SB seasons for Tampa Bay before his trade to Seattle. His combination of power, speed, and clutch performance has made him a fantasy mainstay, and Portland relies on his stolen base contributions and run-scoring to stay competitive in those categories. His Mariners move suppresses some power but the speed travels anywhere.

668390.name: Cole Winn
668390.recentAnalysis: 2026 season: 5.59 ERA/1.50 WHIP, 23 K · currently on D15 IL

668674.name: Lucas Erceg
668674.background: The closer for the Delmarva Emus and a power reliever with a premium fastball, Lucas Erceg has carved out a save situation in Oakland's bullpen with his ability to generate strikeouts and miss bats in the highest-leverage spots. The Emus have committed to his save opportunities as part of their multi-pronged late-game strategy, and his velocity and secondaries give him the ceiling of a legitimate double-digit save contributor. Delmarva's bullpen depth is one of their core CBA strengths.

668678.name: Zac Gallen
668678.background: A reliable mid-rotation starter for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Zac Gallen has been one of the better groundball pitchers in the NL for the Diamondbacks, combining exceptional command with a five-pitch arsenal that keeps hitters off balance throughout games. He was a key contributor to Arizona's surprise World Series run and demonstrated big-game ability as well as regular-season consistency. Syracuse counts on his durability and steady ratios to anchor the middle of their staff week to week.

668709.name: JJ Bleday
668709.background: JJ Bleday was the fourth overall pick in 2019 out of Vanderbilt and struggled in his first MLB stints with Miami, but has reinvented himself as a patient, power-oriented outfielder with Oakland. His plus walk rate and improving contact quality make him a solid fantasy contributor when he gets consistent playing time, though his production is heavily tied to health and lineup positioning in the Athletics' rebuild.

668715.name: Spencer Steer
668715.background: A free agent utility player in CBA with multi-position eligibility, Spencer Steer has shown solid power upside during his Cincinnati tenure — posting 20-plus homer seasons with a pull-heavy approach that generates hard contact at a useful rate. Available for any CBA team looking for corner infield depth, he profiles as a .255-hitting multi-position bat with 20-HR upside who contributes counting stats without being a liability in on-base categories.

668731.recentAnalysis: currently on RM IL — no 2026 stats yet.

Meade Folksy Ferrets, Bryan Reynolds is one of the most underrated outfielders in baseball — a switch-hitter who consistently posts .270-plus averages with 20-plus homers and excellent walk rates for Pittsburgh. His patient approach and ability to hit to all fields make him a model of offensive efficiency, and Ft. Meade has relied on his steady production to stay competitive in average, runs, and power categories year after year.

Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a .295-hitting multi-position contributor whose extraordinary patience at the plate generates walks at an elite rate. His OBP profile and positional flexibility make him one of the more underrated available free agents.

668901.name: Mark Vientos
668901.background: A free agent third baseman in CBA with big power upside, Mark Vientos has shown the raw strength and bat speed to be a legitimate 25-HR bat during his Mets opportunities — featuring impressive exit velocities and the ability to drive the ball out to all fields. Available for any CBA team looking for corner infield power depth, he profiles as a high-variance 20-HR bat who could hit .260 with 25 homers if given consistent playing time.

668904.name: Royce Lewis
668904.background: One of the most explosive young shortstops in baseball, Royce Lewis is a power-speed threat whose career has been repeatedly derailed by torn ACLs — he missed most of 2022 and all of 2023. When healthy he has shown legitimate top-10 fantasy upside, hitting .300+ with 20+ HR pace in limited looks, and he's shifted to third base for Minnesota. His floor is essentially zero given the injury history, but his ceiling when on the field is elite.

668909.name: Gavin Williams
668909.background: The ace of the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms rotation and one of their most prized rotation pieces, Gavin Williams features a devastating fastball-curveball combination that generates elite strikeout rates for Cleveland. His power stuff and ability to miss bats deep into games has made him one of the AL's more exciting young starters, and the Fuzzy Bottoms secured him as a keeper knowing he had genuine top-of-the-rotation upside. North Georgia is getting ace production from one of CBA's more underrated pitching investments.

668930.name: Brice Turang
668930.background: Brice Turang is an energetic second baseman for the Brewers who has developed into a multi-category fantasy asset, combining stolen base ability with surprising pop and an excellent approach at the plate. The Syracuse Sky Chiefs have him slotted in their infield alongside Perdomo and Judge, giving them a middle infield that can impact all five statistical categories. Turang's speed and emerging power make him one of the more versatile pieces on the Sky Chiefs' roster.

668939.name: Adley Rutschman
668939.background: A former top overall draft pick and cornerstone of the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Adley Rutschman is one of the most complete catchers in baseball — combining elite defensive skills with a switch-hitting approach that generates .280-plus averages, excellent walk rates, and solid power from behind the plate. The Fuzzy Bottoms secured him as a marquee keeper knowing his combination of catcher eligibility and offensive production is essentially unmatched at the position in CBA. When healthy, he's the gold standard for fantasy catchers.

668941.name: JoJo Romero
668941.background: A lefty setup specialist in the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets bullpen, JoJo Romero has become a trusted high-leverage arm who generates excellent strikeout rates and keeps left-handed hitters completely off balance. The Ferrets have used him consistently in the seventh and eighth innings to protect leads, and his holds production has been a meaningful part of their weekly CBA strategy. He contributes the kind of steady, unflashy relief value that adds up meaningfully over a long CBA season.

669016.name: Brandon Marsh

The North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms have him in their outfield mix alongside Betts and Burleson, counting on his extra-base hit production and improved plate approach to provide consistent value in the middle of their lineup. Marsh's power and improving contact give the Fuzzy Bottoms a durable everyday outfielder.

669022.name: MacKenzie Gore
669022.background: A left-handed starter for the Nationals who arrived with first-overall-pick pedigree and a power arsenal — mid-90s fastball, elite curveball, and developing changeup — MacKenzie Gore has flashed genuine ace potential while navigating the development bumps typical of young power arms. Available in CBA, he profiles as a high-ceiling SP with legitimate top-of-rotation upside if his command and changeup continue to develop.

669061.name: Gavin Lux
669061.background: A former top prospect for the Dodgers, Gavin Lux is a left-handed hitting second baseman with a solid approach and gap power who has dealt with a torn ACL and inconsistent playing time. Now with Cincinnati, he has a chance at regular at-bats. He's a 15-HR, 10-SB type who contributes batting average and rarely hurts ratios.

The Bristol Banshees have him as depth outfield insurance, knowing that when Stowers gets hot he can provide a powerful bat that outperforms his draft position. His raw power and improving contact make him a high-upside depth piece for the Banshees.

669127.name: Shea Langeliers
669127.background: Shea Langeliers is a power-hitting catcher for the Athletics who has developed into one of baseball's best offensive backstops, combining plus raw power with a solid contact approach and excellent throw rates that make him valuable in both standard and traditional fantasy formats. The Sugar Land Space Cowboys have him as their primary catcher alongside Cruz and Lindor, giving them premium offensive production from a position that often fails to deliver big fantasy numbers. 669194.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with solid mid-rotation upside, Ryne Nelson has shown flashes of an effective power sinker approach with Arizona that generates groundballs and keeps the ball in the park. He's a streaming-friendly option rather than a featured starter, but there's ceiling if his command sharpens.
