670280.name: David Bednar
670280.background: The closer for the Sugar Land Space Cowboys and one of the better save sources in the NL, David Bednar has been Pittsburgh's primary ninth-inning option for multiple years — a power reliever with a premium fastball and plus slider who generates strikeouts and converts saves at a reliable rate. The Space Cowboys have leveraged his save totals as a consistent CBA advantage, and his durability makes him a trustworthy bullpen anchor week to week. Sugar Land's rotation and bullpen depth have made them one of the more complete CBA rosters.

670329.name: Rico Garcia
670329.background: A high-leverage setup reliever for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Rico Garcia has become one of the most effective middle relievers in the NL with a deceptive delivery and plus secondary stuff that generates strikeouts and keeps inherited runners from scoring. The Fuzzy Bottoms have used him as a key bridge to their closer, and his holds production has been a meaningful part of North Georgia's dominant early-season relief performance. He's a quiet contributor who helps the Fuzzy Bottoms win pitching-heavy CBA weeks.

670623.name: Isaac Paredes
670623.background: Isaac Paredes is a disciplined third baseman/DH now with the Astros who has established himself as a valuable fantasy contributor through his exceptional plate patience and surprising power, annually posting on-base percentages north of .360. The Portland Chinook have him as a lineup complement alongside Turner and Olson, trusting his walk rate and improving power to produce solid counting stats in a productive Houston lineup. Paredes' on-base ability and power make him a quiet but reliable contributor for the Chinook.

670770.name: TJ Friedl
670770.background: A speedy outfielder for the Reds whose elite sprint speed and developing contact game make him one of the most valuable pure stolen base options in fantasy baseball when he's getting consistent at-bats, TJ Friedl combines his plus wheels with improving on-base skills to create genuine multi-category value. Available in CBA, he profiles as a speed-first contributor with 25-SB upside and improving batting average who can change the complexion of a fantasy week.

The Warren Whistlepigs have him as their primary catcher alongside their deep lineup, knowing that premium offensive production from the catcher position is a rare and valuable commodity in the CBA. 695505.background: One of the Sugar Land Space Cowboys' most exciting young arms, Chase Burns arrived as the Reds' most prized pitching prospect with a power arsenal featuring a triple-digit fastball and a devastating slider that generates elite whiff rates. His rapid ascent through Cincinnati's system and immediate MLB success demonstrated that his raw stuff translates at the highest level, and he's quickly become one of the NL's more exciting young starters. The Space Cowboys are betting on him developing into a genuine frontline ace.

695506.name: Jac Caglianone
695506.background: One of the Manhattan Mega Rats' most intriguing young position player investments, Jac Caglianone arrived as a college two-way player whose bat-first skills — enormous raw power, strong athleticism, and advanced feel for hitting — convinced the Mega Rats to prioritize him as a position player keeper. His enormous frame and elite bat speed project toward plus power at maturity, and Manhattan is betting he develops into one of the CBA's more dangerous left-handed bats. He's a long-term investment with a genuinely loud ceiling.

695562.name: Eric Wagaman
695562.background: A first baseman-corner infielder for Miami, Eric Wagaman is a big left-handed bat who has shown good power in the minors and is looking to establish himself in the major leagues. He profiles as a power-over-average type who could be a useful depth piece.

695657.name: Colson Montgomery

Available to any CBA team willing to make the investment, he profiles as a .270-hitting shortstop with 20-HR upside once he reaches his ceiling. His elite walk rates and improving contact make him a compelling pickup for any roster in need of shortstop help.

695734.name: Daylen Lile
695734.background: Daylen Lile is a toolsy young outfielder for the Nationals who rose quickly through the system behind strong contact skills and promising athleticism, earning his way onto Washington's big-league roster as an everyday contributor. The Portland Chinook grabbed him as one of their exciting youth investments, pairing him with Trea Turner and Cole Ragans for a well-rounded roster. Lile's ability to make contact and put balls in play makes him a reliable counting-stats producer for the Chinook.

696030.name: Alejandro Osuna
696030.background: Alejandro Osuna is a young outfield prospect in the Texas Rangers system who reached the majors as a high-contact bat with line-drive approach. He doesn't project for big power but could contribute in average and on-base percentage categories as he develops, and his speed adds a stolen base upside that gives him fantasy utility if he secures a full-time role.

696100.name: Hunter Goodman
696100.background: A power-hitting catcher on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster, Hunter Goodman emerged from the Rockies' system with one of the better raw power tools among catching prospects, generating impressive exit velocities and home run distances in his early MLB opportunities. The Ferrets secured him as a developmental catcher keep with the upside of a 25-HR backstop if his contact skills develop. At Coors Field he has one of the best home run-friendly environments in baseball to help him post counting stats.

696131.name: Mason Black
696131.background: Mason Black is a highly touted pitching prospect for the San Francisco Giants who was one of the top arms in the 2022 draft. He features a power sinker-slider combination and exceptional groundball tendencies, making him particularly valuable in parks like Oracle Field. His major league sample has been limited, but his ceiling is that of a mid-rotation starter with strong ratios.

696149.name: Bubba Chandler
696149.background: One of the Manhattan Mega Rats' most intriguing young rotation pieces, Bubba Chandler is a two-way prospect who Pittsburgh developed as a starting pitcher after he showed premium arm talent that was too good to pass up. His mid-90s fastball and advanced secondary stuff give him genuine ace upside, and the Mega Rats grabbed him as a keeper betting on his development trajectory continuing upward. If his command refines, he could become one of the better young starters in CBA.

696285.name: Jacob Young
696285.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with solid speed, Jacob Young has shown the athleticism and contact skills to contribute as an everyday outfielder for Washington — bringing stolen base ability and decent batting average from the outfield position. Available for any CBA team looking for speed-focused outfield depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting OF with double-digit stolen base upside when given consistent playing time.

697553.name: Carson Palmquist
697553.background: A left-handed pitching prospect for Colorado, Carson Palmquist was a top-100 prospect who features a plus curveball and good feel for pitching. The Coors Field environment makes his fantasy value complicated — away starts are viable streaming options while home starts are typically high-risk.

He's quickly become one of the most exciting young arms in all of CBA.

801403.name: Chase Dollander
